definition:
1. commensurate with  another thing, (Euripides): exactly fitting , (Aeschulus Tragicus); τῷδε τἀνδρὶ ξ. being of like age with , (Sophocles Tragicus); ποίᾳ σύμμετρος τύχ; coincident with  what chance? i. e. in the very nick of time, (Sophocles Tragicus); see. infr. III. 2. 
2. commensurable , (Aristotle Philosopher) 
3. in measure with, proportionable, exactly suitable , (Isocrates Orator), etc. 
4. absolute in right measure, in due proportion, symmetrical , opposite to ὑπερβάλλων and ἐλλείπων, (Plato Philosophus), etc. 
5. generally, fitting, meet, due , (Aeschulus Tragicus);—; σύμμετρος ὡς κλύειν within fit distance  for hearing, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
6. adverb -τρως, (Isocrates Orator), etc. 
7. in due time , (Euripides) (ML)
